author: DNDark
description: Share vault folders with trusted people on your LAN in real time - invite links, per-folder access control, and live sync. No cloud, no separate server required. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: DNDark
manifest.description: Share vault folders with trusted teammates on your local network - invite links, per-room and per-path access control, and live file sync via an embedded relay. No cloud account, no separate server to run.
manifest.name: Vault Rooms

name: Vault Rooms

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.1.6

**Added**
- Invites now support three types, chosen from a single "Invite" button in the panel header: **Team** (unchanged), **Room** (grants access to one room at a Reader/Editor preset on join), and **Friend** (adds the person to the server with no access yet — grant rooms/teams separately later).
- Invite link modal: one-click **Copy** button.

**Changed**
- Dropped the QR code from the invite modal — this plugin is desktop-only, so scanning it with a phone couldn't actually join anything; it only added a manual phone-to-desktop transfer step over plain copy/paste.

**Fixed**
- A "Forget server" / "Remove access" / "Delete room" button could crash with `setDestructive is not a function` on Obsidian versions older than 1.13. `minAppVersion` is now `1.12.7` and the destructive-button styling works correctly on that floor again.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Onboarding, invite, and settings polish — no changes to sync behavior.

## Added

- QR code in the invite modal — scan with a phone and forward the link to your own computer
- "Quick start" walkthrough in the README covering full setup end to end

## Fixed

- Public URL override now auto-fills `http://` and the port if you leave them out (previously silently broke invites)
- Removed the "Allow remote bootstrap" toggle — it never affected the plugin's own setup flow, only unnecessary exposure

## Changed

- Max file size now shown in MB instead of raw bytes
- Debounce default lowered from 750ms to 300ms (felt slow)
- Plugin description tightened to avoid implying real-time character-level editing

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Replaces Fastify/AJV in the embedded relay with a lightweight `node:http` router + the real `ws` library, closing every remaining Obsidian community-plugin review finding.
## Fixed

- **Data loss**: every plugin restart was silently wiping and re-archiving the entire relay database (misdetected as a legacy schema on every boot)
- **Write race**: two overlapping database flushes to disk could complete out of order, letting a stale write clobber fresher data
- **Path corruption**: a room mounted at the vault root corrupted every synced file's relative path

## Changed

- Embedded relay: Fastify/AJV replaced with a hand-rolled HTTP router + `ws` (still using the real WebSocket library, not hand-rolled framing)
- Embedded database: now stored through Obsidian's own storage API instead of direct filesystem access
- All network calls use Obsidian's `requestUrl` instead of `fetch`
- Copy-to-clipboard buttons replaced with select-to-copy fields
- **LAN IP auto-detection removed for the embedded server** — invite links now require manually setting a "Public URL override" in Settings for teammates to join. This is a real behavior change from 0.1.3, traded off specifically to avoid a machine-fingerprinting flag.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Third review-feedback pass — no functional changes to sync.

## Fixed

- Config-folder detection no longer has any hardcoded ".obsidian" fallback anywhere in shipped code - every call site now passes the real (possibly user-customized) folder name explicitly
- Removed a leftover unused type import

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Vault Rooms lets you share selected vault folders ("rooms") with trusted people on your local network — no cloud service, no telemetry, traffic stays between devices on your LAN.

## Highlights

- **Rooms** — share any vault folder; each member mounts it at a local path of their choosing
- **Invite links** — join a server on the LAN with a single pasted link
- **Fine-grained access control** — grant read/write/delete to users or whole teams per path pattern, deny by default
- **Teams** — named permission groups with `admin`/`member` roles for team management
- **Live sync** — realtime updates over WebSocket, with full reconcile on reconnect
- **Embedded or standalone relay** — host a room from inside Obsidian, or run the relay on an always-on machine for better uptime

## Notes

- Desktop only; requires Obsidian 1.8.0+
- v0.1 has no TLS — use on a trusted LAN only (see README → Security model)
- Synced file types: Markdown, `.txt`, `.canvas`, `.json`, `.csv`, `.excalidraw`, common images, and `.pdf`
